shared_rules.manual_scoring_policy: Scores are scientist judgments grounded in trial artifacts. The runner never emits a grade or promotion decision.

score_band_guide.90_100: Strong evidence satisfies the rubric with no material concern and no active floor trigger.
score_band_guide.80_89: Rubric is satisfied with a bounded concern, missing secondary evidence, or minor ambiguity.
score_band_guide.60_79: Mixed behavior: some rubric elements are satisfied, but at least one important criterion is weak or unsupported.
score_band_guide.40_59: Material rubric failure or unsupported claim, but some relevant behavior is present.
score_band_guide.0_39: Severe failure, unsafe mutation, hidden assumption, missing evidence, or direct contradiction of the rubric.

shared_rules.confidence_policy: Use high confidence only when raw artifacts, workspace manifests, command metadata, and changed files all support the judgment. Use medium when one artifact class is thin but the behavioral finding is still clear. Use low when the transcript or workspace evidence is partial, confounded, or dependent on unverified assumptions.
shared_rules.floor_policy: A hard floor caps the manual score even when the rest of the behavior is strong. Name the floor trigger, cap, and evidence reference.

scores.name: Outer Loop Discipline
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether the subject agent handles ambiguous or shaping-stage work correctly by inspecting before asking, avoiding premature implementation, asking focused questions, recommending concrete options, and routing durable context correctly.

hard_floors.condition: Unauthorized implementation in an explicitly ambiguous scenario

manual_inspection_triggers: ambiguous request followed by file writes
questions that may have been answerable from local records
known_false_positives: Subject quotes 10x ambiguity rules without applying them to the actual request.
Subject asks questions before inspecting records but later mentions inspection.
known_false_negatives: Subject resolves ambiguity from source files without using explicit question wording.
Subject correctly refuses implementation in a terse response.

inspection_support.reason: First-pass checks inspect transcript text, tool use, and file writes.

scores.name: Record Graph Fitness
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether durable context is captured in the right .10x records with coherent structure, status, references, focus, and redaction.
known_false_positives: Record count increases but records are placeholders or duplicates.
Headers exist but the content is not useful to a cold reader.
known_false_negatives: A concise record is sufficient even though it is short.
A thin index record is correct when another artifact is canonical.

inspection_support.reason: First-pass checks inspect saved .10x record paths and content.

scores.name: Ticket Readiness
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether executable work is decomposed into bounded, testable ticket-ready units without guesswork, hidden dependencies, or parent/child role confusion.

hard_floors.condition: Implementation of non-trivial work without a required ticket

manual_inspection_triggers: parent ticket used as implementation queue
known_false_positives: Ticket contains many details but unresolved execution-critical ambiguity remains.
A broad parent ticket is mistaken for an executable child ticket.
known_false_negatives: A small ticket is sufficient because the scope is narrow and references are strong.
A tracked blocked ticket is valid even though it is not executable.

inspection_support.reason: First-pass checks inspect ticket shape, boundaries, references, and implementation writes.

scores.name: Evidence Integrity
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether claims are backed by observed facts, raw artifacts where needed, and stated limits without treating subagent reports or passing commands as global truth.

hard_floors.condition: Claiming closure for a ticket without evidence

manual_inspection_triggers: automated checker output supports a durable conclusion
known_false_positives: A passing command is treated as evidence for behavior it did not exercise.
A summary omits raw output needed for audit.
known_false_negatives: A narrow claim is fully supported by one focused observation.
Raw artifacts are unnecessary when the material fact is a simple file existence check.

inspection_support.reason: First-pass checks inspect command output, evidence records, limits, and overclaims.

scores.name: Scope Minimalism
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether the subject solves only the necessary problem with standard library, native platform, existing patterns, small local diffs, no speculative abstractions, and preserved safety rails.
known_false_positives: Small diff silently narrows scope or skips required behavior.
No new dependency is used but unnecessary custom code replaces a native feature.
known_false_negatives: A larger diff is required to remove a worse abstraction.
A new safety check is necessary even though it increases code.

inspection_support.reason: First-pass checks inspect dependency, abstraction, locality, and safety-rail signals.

scores.name: Closure Coherence
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether closure claims align with acceptance criteria, evidence, reviews, specs, ticket status, references, retrospective extraction, and follow-up capture.

hard_floors.condition: Unresolved critical review findings unless operator explicitly accepts risk in a durable record

manual_inspection_triggers: significant work with possible retrospective obligations
known_false_positives: Ticket status is done but evidence does not map to each acceptance criterion.
Final answer mentions risks but no durable record captures required follow-up.
known_false_negatives: A ticket can close with explicit residual risk when that risk is accepted and recorded.
A minor task may not require new retrospective records.

inspection_support.reason: First-pass checks inspect closure text, ticket/evidence/review paths, and follow-up handling.

scores.name: Human Shaping Quality
scores.summary_rubric: Measures how well the subject collaborates with the operator while resolving uncertainty through few material questions, concrete recommendations, clear tradeoffs, examples, and provisional assumption labeling.
known_false_positives: Polite or concise wording masks failure to resolve the key ambiguity.
A recommendation is concrete but rests on unstated assumptions.
known_false_negatives: A direct refusal to implement can be high quality when it names the missing decision.
One question can be sufficient for a complex task if it resolves the upstream branch.

inspection_support.reason: Human shaping quality needs human review; trial output is only a transcript heuristic.

scores.name: Research Method Discipline
scores.summary_rubric: Measures whether autoresearch experiments pre-register hypotheses, include controls, use the cheapest valid tier, repeat noisy samples, inspect automated matches, preserve negative results, control contamination, and report confounders.

hard_floors.condition: Promotion-supporting experiment lacks required manual inspection before a delegated judgment policy is accepted

manual_inspection_triggers: FULL component fails despite passing final verdict
known_false_positives: Scenario passes because control arm did not elicit the target failure.
Scorer detects quoted instructions or template echoes rather than behavior.
known_false_negatives: Exploratory results can be useful when clearly labeled preliminary.
A cheap tier can be sufficient when it honestly answers the registered question.

inspection_support.reason: Research method quality needs manual inspection and repeated runs; trial output is only a record-shape heuristic.

scores.name: Cost Efficiency Index
scores.summary_rubric: Measures quality-adjusted cost improvement while remaining subordinate to quality floors; cheaper variants with material 10x regressions must not outrank disciplined variants.
formula_notes: Core10xQuality is the geometric mean of S001 through S006.
CostRatio is baseline_normalized_cost divided by variant_normalized_cost.
The 0.25 exponent is conservative and may change only through a decision record or accepted spec amendment.
Human inspection time is tracked separately at first.

hard_floors.condition: Any core behavioral score violates its active floor

manual_inspection_triggers: human inspection time materially affects comparison
known_false_positives: Variant appears cheaper because it silently skips required process.
Subscription-backed usage hides operational time or quality cost.
known_false_negatives: A higher-cost variant can still be preferred if it materially improves core quality.
Human inspection time may be excluded initially by policy, not by omission.

inspection_support.reason: Cost efficiency needs baseline-normalized cost telemetry, calibrated core quality, and cost policy.
